memberdef.argsstring: (const float *q, const float *k, const float *v, float *output, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim)
memberdef.name: attention_forward_causal_head_major_gqa_flash

detaileddescription.para:
Flash attention forward for GQA (prefill, no score materialization) Test
test_flash_attention.py::TestFlashAttention::test_flash_forward
test_flash_attention.py::TestFlashAttention::test_flash_vs_score_matrix
test_flash_attention.py::TestFlashAttention::test_flash_gqa
test_attention.py::TestAttentionForward::test_flash_forward
Online softmax with streaming KV. O(N) memory instead of O(N^2). For prefill: all tokens attend to previous tokens.
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *q, const float *k, const float *v, float *output, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int kv_stride_tokens)

Flash attention forward with custom KV stride (for KV cache) Test
test_flash_attention.py::TestFlashAttention::test_flash_strided

Variant with configurable kv_stride_tokens for KV cache layouts where K/V may not be contiguous in memory.
After changes: make test

memberdef.name: attention_forward_decode_head_major_gqa_regular

briefdescription.para: WARNING: This is NOT true flash attention!

detaileddescription.para: This function is named "flash" but implements regular attention with O(n) complexity. It's kept for reference and as a fallback.

TRUE flash attention is implemented in attention_flash_true.c Test
test_kv_cache_attention.py::TestKVCacheAttention::test_regular_decode
test_attention.py::TestAttentionForward::test_regular_decode
Regular attention decode (score-matrix version) for fallback.
After changes: make test

briefdescription.para: Fused MLP (Gate + Up + SwiGLU + Down) for prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Tiles along token dimension to keep gate/up/hidden in L3 cache.

memberdef.name: fused_mlp_swiglu_prefill_w1w2_quant

briefdescription.para: Quantized fused MLP for prefill (W1=gate+up, W2=down)

detaileddescription.para: W1 uses Q8_0 activations (Q5_0/Q8_0 weights), W2 uses Q8_K activations (Q4_K/Q6_K weights).
Uses Q8_0 activations for W1 (Q5_0/Q8_0 weights) and Q8_K activations for W2 (Q4_K/Q6_K weights).

memberdef.argsstring: (int aligned_embed_dim, int aligned_intermediate_dim)

briefdescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_mlp_swiglu_prefill_w1w2_quant.

memberdef.argsstring: (int intermediate)
memberdef.name: fused_mlp_swiglu_scratch_size

briefdescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_mlp_swiglu_prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_mlp_swiglu_prefill.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, const float *gamma, const float *Wq, const float *Wk, const float *Wv, float *Q, float *K, float *V, int seq_len, int hidden, int q_dim, int kv_dim, float eps, float *scratch)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill

briefdescription.para: Fused RMSNorm + QKV projection for prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Tiles along token dimension to keep intermediate x_norm in L2 cache. Avoids ~7MB DRAM traffic per layer for seq_len=1024, hidden=896.

scratch
Temporary buffer from fused_rmsnorm_qkv_scratch_size()
Fused RMSNorm + QKV projection for prefill.
KEY INSIGHT: For Qwen2-0.5B, all QKV weights fit in L3: Wq (896×896) + Wk (128×896) + Wv (128×896) = 4.1MB < 6MB L3

So we use M-tiling (tokens) only:
For each token tile: a. Compute RMSNorm ONCE into scratch (x_norm stays in L2) b. Do all three GEMMs (Q, K, V) against cached x_norm c. Weights stay hot in L3 across all token tiles

This avoids both:
Large x_norm intermediate buffer (only TILE_M × hidden in L2)
RMSNorm recomputation (done once per token tile, used 3×)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, const float *gamma, const float *Wq, const float *Bq, const float *Wk, const float *Bk, const float *Wv, const float *Bv, float *Q, float *K, float *V, int seq_len, int embed_dim, int aligned_embed_dim, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int kv_stride_tokens, float eps, float *scratch)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill_head_major

briefdescription.para: Fused RMSNorm + QKV projection for prefill (head-major outputs)

detaileddescription.para: Writes Q as [num_heads, seq_len, aligned_head_dim] and K/V with stride kv_stride_tokens for KV-cache compatibility.
Q is written as [num_heads, seq_len, aligned_head_dim]. K/V are written with kv_stride_tokens for KV-cache compatibility.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, const float *gamma, const void *Wq, const float *Bq, CKDataType wq_dt, const void *Wk, const float *Bk, CKDataType wk_dt, const void *Wv, const float *Bv, CKDataType wv_dt, float *Q, float *K, float *V, int seq_len, int embed_dim, int aligned_embed_dim, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int kv_stride_tokens, float eps, void *scratch)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill_head_major_quant

briefdescription.para: Fused RMSNorm + QKV projection for prefill (head-major, Q8 activations)

detaileddescription.para: Supports Q5_0 or Q8_0 weights with Q8_0 activations.
Supports Q5_0 or Q8_0 weights with Q8_0 activations. Writes K/V directly into KV cache layout (kv_stride_tokens).

memberdef.argsstring: (int aligned_embed_dim)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill_head_major_quant_scratch_size

briefdescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill_head_major_quant.

memberdef.argsstring: (int hidden)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv_scratch_size

briefdescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for fused_rmsnorm_qkv_prefill.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, size_t n)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, size_t n)
memberdef.name: gelu_backward_fast

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const uint16_t *d_output, uint16_t *d_input, size_t n, float *scratch_input, float *scratch_d_output, float *scratch_d_input)
memberdef.name: gelu_backward_fast_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, size_t n)
memberdef.name: gelu_backward_scalar

memberdef.argsstring: (float *data, size_t n)
memberdef.name: gelu_exact_inplace

memberdef.argsstring: (float *data, size_t n)
memberdef.name: gelu_fast_inplace

detaileddescription.para:
GELU activation forward (fast approximation, in-place) Test
test_gelu.py::TestGELUForward::test_gelu_fast_inplace
test_gelu.py::TestGELUForward::test_gelu_vs_exact
test_parity.py::test_gelu_parity
Fast GELU approximation: 0.5 * x * (1 + tanh(sqrt(2/pi) * (x + 0.044715 * x^3))) In-place on contiguous buffer.
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t *data, size_t n, float *scratch)
memberdef.name: gelu_fast_inplace_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const float *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q4_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-matrix multiply: C[M,N] = A[M,K] @ B[N,K]^T + bias.

parameterdescription.para: Input matrix [M x K], row-major FP32
Weight matrix in Q4_0 format, [N x K] stored row-major
Optional bias [N], NULL if not used
Output dimension (number of rows in B)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q4_1

briefdescription.para: GEMM with transposed Q4_1 weights: C = A @ B^T.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations [M x K], row-major FP32
Weight matrix in Q4_1 format [N x K], row-major quantized
Optional bias [N], NULL if not used

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q4_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_1

briefdescription.para: GEMM with transposed Q5_1 weights: C = A @ B^T.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations [M x K], row-major FP32
Weight matrix in Q5_1 format [N x K], row-major quantized
Optional bias [N], NULL if not used

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q6_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q6_k_q8_k

briefdescription.para: NT GEMM: C = A @ B^T where A is Q8_K and B is Q6_K.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the typical inference pattern:
A: Activations in Q8_K format [M x K]
B: Weights in Q6_K format [N x K]
C: Output [M x N]

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-matrix multiply: C[M,N] = A[M,K] @ B[N,K]^T + bias.

parameterdescription.para: Input matrix [M x K], row-major FP32
Weight matrix in Q8_0 format, [N x K] stored row-major
Optional bias [N], NULL if not used
Output dimension (number of rows in B)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: gemm_nt_q8_0_q8_0 with optional bias (matches header signature)

detaileddescription.para: C[m,n] = A[m,K] @ B[n,K]^T + bias[n]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *Y, const void *W, const float *X, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMM based on available SIMD.

memberdef.name: gemm_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *Y, const void *W, const float *X, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_q6_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *Y, const void *W, const void *X_q8, int M, int N, int K)

briefdescription.para: GEMM: Y = W @ X^T where W is Q6_K and X is Q8_K.

parameterdescription.para: Output matrix [N x M] in row-major
Weight matrix in Q6_K format [M x K]
Input matrix in Q8_K format [N x K]

memberdef.name: gemv_q4_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV based on available SIMD.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k_parallel

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k_parallel_simd

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k_ref

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV for Q5_0 weights based on CPU features.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority (best available):
AVX-512 (512-bit vectors) - Intel Skylake-X+
AVX2+FMA (256-bit vectors) - Intel Haswell+
AVX (256-bit vectors) - Intel Sandy Bridge+
SSE4.1 (128-bit vectors) - Intel Nehalem+
Reference (scalar) - Fallback
Uses ck_features.h for standardized feature detection.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_parallel

briefdescription.para: Parallel reference GEMV for Q5_0 × FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_parallel_simd

briefdescription.para: Parallel SIMD GEMV for Q5_0 × FP32 with prefetching.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 input.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q6_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q6_k_q8_k

briefdescription.para: GEMV: y = W @ x where W is Q6_K and x is Q8_K.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q6_k_q8_k_parallel

briefdescription.para: Parallel reference GEMV for Q6_K × Q8_K.

detaileddescription.para: Caller provides ith (thread index) and nth (total threads). Each thread processes rows [r0, r1).

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q6_k_q8_k_parallel_simd

briefdescription.para: Parallel SIMD GEMV for Q6_K × Q8_K.

detaileddescription.para: Uses best available SIMD (AVX/SSE) with row prefetching. Caller provides ith/nth from OpenMP region.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV for Q8_0 weights based on CPU features.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority (best available):
AVX-512 (512-bit vectors) - Intel Skylake-X+
AVX2+FMA (256-bit vectors) - Intel Haswell+
AVX (256-bit vectors) - Intel Sandy Bridge+
SSE4.1 (128-bit vectors) - Intel Nehalem+
Reference (scalar) - Fallback
Uses ck_features.h for standardized feature detection.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q8_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q8_0 weights and Q8_0 input.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q8_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *image, float *patches, int C, int H, int W, int P)
memberdef.name: im2patch

detaileddescription.para: im2patch: Transforms an image into a sequence of flattened patches.
Image Layout: [C, H, W] (Row-major: W is fastest moving) Output Layout: [num_patches, C * P * P]
num_patches = (H/P) * (W/P) P = patch_size

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *image, uint16_t *patches, int C, int H, int W, int P)
memberdef.name: im2patch_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *buf, int num_heads, int tokens, int cache_capacity, int aligned_head_dim)

memberdef.name: kv_cache_store

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *__restrict k_token, const float *__restrict v_token, float *__restrict k_cache, float *__restrict v_cache, int num_kv_heads, int token_index, int cache_capacity, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim)
memberdef.name: kv_cache_write_head_major

memberdef.name: layernorm_forward_unrolled_slice_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, const float *beta, float *output, float *mean_cache, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, const float *beta, float *output, float *mean_cache, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, float eps)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *W_fc1, const float *b_fc1, const float *W_fc2, const float *b_fc2, float *fc1_output, float *output, int T, int aligned_dim, int num_threads)
memberdef.name: mlp_token_parallel

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const uint16_t *W_fc1, const uint16_t *b_fc1, const uint16_t *W_fc2, const uint16_t *b_fc2, float *fc1_output, float *output, int T, int aligned_dim, int num_threads, float *scratch_bias1_f, float *scratch_bias2_f, uint16_t *scratch_fc1_bf16)
memberdef.name: mlp_token_parallel_bf16

detaileddescription.para: Optimized MLP Forward (BF16 weights, FP32 activations)
Caller-provided scratch buffers: scratch_bias1_f: [4*D] floats scratch_bias2_f: [D] floats scratch_fc1_bf16: [T * 4*D] uint16_t (BF16)

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const uint16_t *W_fc1, const uint16_t *b_fc1, const uint16_t *W_fc2, const uint16_t *b_fc2, float *fc1_output, float *output, int T, int aligned_dim, int num_threads, float *scratch_input_f, float *scratch_bias1_f, float *scratch_bias2_f, uint16_t *scratch_fc1_bf16)
memberdef.name: mlp_token_parallel_bf16_fp32act

detaileddescription.para: Alternative: Fully FP32 activations throughout
Caller-provided scratch buffers: scratch_input_f: [T * D] floats scratch_bias1_f: [4*D] floats scratch_bias2_f: [D] floats scratch_fc1_bf16: [T * 4*D] uint16_t (BF16)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *W_fc1, const float *b_fc1, const float *W_fc2, const float *b_fc2, float *fc1_output, float *output, int T, int aligned_dim, int num_threads)
memberdef.name: mlp_token_parallel_exact

memberdef.argsstring: (float *output, const float *expert_output, float routing_weight, int hidden_dim)
memberdef.name: moe_accumulate_expert_f32

briefdescription.para: Accumulate expert output: output += routing_weight * expert_output.

parameterdescription.para: Token output buffer [hidden_dim], accumulated in place
Softmax routing weight for this expert

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *d_patches, float *d_image, int C, int H, int W, int P)
memberdef.name: patch2im

detaileddescription.para: patch2im: Accumulates gradients from patches back into the image. (Backward pass)
d_patches: [num_patches, C * P * P] d_image: [C, H, W] (Accumulated)

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *d_patches, uint16_t *d_image, int C, int H, int W, int P)
memberdef.name: patch2im_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *y, int num_rows, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_batch_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Batch quantize FP32 to Q8_0 format (row-major output)

detaileddescription.para: Quantizes multiple rows of FP32 data to Q8_0 format, placing each row's Q8_0 output at the correct byte offset for GEMM compatibility.
Memory layout: Input: [num_rows, k] FP32, row-major (stride = k * sizeof(float)) Output: [num_rows, q8_row_bytes] Q8_0, row-major (stride = q8_row_bytes)
where q8_row_bytes = (k / 32) * sizeof(block_q8_0) = (k / 32) * 34

parameterdescription.para: Elements per row (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *y, int num_rows, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_batch_q8_k

briefdescription.para: Batch quantize FP32 to Q8_K format (row-major output)

detaileddescription.para: Same as quantize_batch_q8_0 but for Q8_K format (super-blocks).

parameterdescription.para: Elements per row (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *y, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_row_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Quantize FP32 to Q8_0 format (scalar reference)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *y, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_row_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_backward

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const uint16_t *d_output, uint16_t *d_input, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_backward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_forward

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, uint16_t *output, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_forward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *data, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_forward_inplace

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t *data, size_t n)
memberdef.name: relu_forward_inplace_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *d_output, const float *input, const float *gamma, const float *rstd_cache, float *d_input, float *d_gamma, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_backward

detaileddescription.para:
RMSNorm backward pass Test
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormBackward::test_backward_tokens
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormBackward::test_backward_single
test_parity.py::test_rmsnorm_backward_parity
Computes dX and dGamma given dY, X, gamma, and cached rstd. dX_i = rstd * (dY_i * gamma_i - x_hat_i * m) dGamma_i = sum_t (dY_i * x_hat_i)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *d_output, const uint16_t *input, const float *gamma, const float *rstd_cache, uint16_t *d_input, float *d_gamma, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_backward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *d_output, const uint8_t *input, const float *gamma, const float *rstd_cache, uint8_t *d_input, float *d_gamma, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float *scratch_d_output, float *scratch_input, float *scratch_d_input)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_backward_int4

memberdef.argsstring: (const int8_t *d_output, const int8_t *input, const float *gamma, const float *rstd_cache, int8_t *d_input, float *d_gamma, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float *scratch_d_output, float *scratch_input, float *scratch_d_input)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_backward_int8

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, float *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_forward

detaileddescription.para:
RMSNorm forward pass Test
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_fp32_tokens
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_fp32_single
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_perf_rolled
test_layernorm.py::TestLayerNormForward::test_rmsnorm_compat
test_parity.py::test_rmsnorm_parity
RMSNorm: y[i] = gamma[i] * x[i] / sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const float *gamma, uint16_t *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_forward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *input, const float *gamma, uint8_t *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps, float *scratch_input, float *scratch_output)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_forward_int4

memberdef.argsstring: (const int8_t *input, const float *gamma, int8_t *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps, float *scratch_input, float *scratch_output)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_forward_int8

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *d_out, float *d_x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_backward

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE backward (inverse rotation) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEBackward::test_rope_backward
test_rope.py::TestRoPEBackward::test_rope_backward_vs_separate
RoPE backward: inverse rotation (rotate by -θ). Since cos(-θ) = cos(θ) and sin(-θ) = -sin(θ): d_x[2i] = d0 * c + d1 * s d_x[2i+1] = -d0 * s + d1 * c
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *d_out, uint16_t *d_x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, float *scratch_d_out, float *scratch_d_x)
memberdef.name: rope_backward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *d_x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_backward_inplace

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE backward in-place (overwrite with inverse rotation) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEBackward::test_rope_backward_inplace
In-place backward: overwrite d_out with inverse-rotated gradients. Useful when d_x == d_out is acceptable (saves memory).
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *d_q_out, const float *d_k_out, float *d_q, float *d_k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_backward_qk

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE backward for both dQ and dK Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEBackward::test_rope_backward_qk
Combined RoPE backward for both dQ and dK gradients.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *d_q_out, const uint16_t *d_k_out, uint16_t *d_q, uint16_t *d_k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, float *scratch_dq_out, float *scratch_dq, float *scratch_dk_out, float *scratch_dk)
memberdef.name: rope_backward_qk_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_forward

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE forward (head-major layout, in-place) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_forward
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_vs_separate
test_parity.py::test_rope_parity
Applies rotary position embeddings in-place to Q or K tensor. x: [num_heads, num_tokens, head_dim] head-major
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t *x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, float *scratch)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_qk

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE forward for both Q and K (common inference pattern) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_forward_qk
test_fused_attention_decode.py::TestFusedAttentionDecode::test_qk_rope
test_parity.py::test_rope_qk_parity
Combined RoPE forward for both Q and K in one call. q: [num_heads, num_tokens, head_dim] k: [num_kv_heads, num_tokens, head_dim]
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t *q, uint16_t *k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, float *scratch_q, float *scratch_k)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_qk_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, int q_stride_tokens, int k_stride_tokens)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_qk_strided

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE forward for both Q and K with custom strides (KV cache layouts) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_forward_qk_strided
test_kv_cache_attention.py::TestKVCacheAttention::test_qk_rope_strided
Combined QK RoPE with configurable strides for KV cache layouts.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (float *x, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset, int head_stride_tokens)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_strided

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE forward with custom head stride (for KV cache layouts) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_strided
test_kv_cache_attention.py::TestKVCacheAttention::test_rope_decode
Variant with configurable head_stride_tokens for non-contiguous head layouts.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (float *cos_cache, float *sin_cache, int max_seq_len, int head_dim, float base)
memberdef.name: rope_precompute_cache

detaileddescription.para:
Precompute RoPE cos/sin cache Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPECache::test_cache_computation
test_rope.py::TestRoPECache::test_cache_values
Precomputes cos(m * theta_i) and sin(m * theta_i) for positions 0..max_seq_len-1. cos_cache, sin_cache: [max_seq_len, head_dim/2]
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const float *x, float alpha, int n)
memberdef.name: scal_copy_f32

briefdescription.para: Scaled copy: y = alpha * x.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, size_t n)

memberdef.argsstring: (float x)
memberdef.name: sigmoid_scalar

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *logits, const int32_t *targets, int tokens, int vocab_size, float *d_logits, float *loss_out)
memberdef.name: softmax_cross_entropy_loss

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *logits, const int32_t *targets, int tokens, int vocab_size, uint16_t *d_logits, float *loss_out, float *scratch_logits, float *scratch_d_logits)
memberdef.name: softmax_cross_entropy_loss_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_backward

detaileddescription.para:
SwiGLU backward pass Test
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUBackward::test_backward_tokens
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUBackward::test_backward_single
test_parity.py::test_swiglu_backward_parity
Computes dGate and dUp given dY. dGate = dy * b * silu'(a), dUp = dy * silu(a)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, const uint16_t *d_output, uint16_t *d_input, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_backward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *d_output, float *d_input, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_backward_exact

detaileddescription.para:
SwiGLU backward pass (exact version using stdlib sigmoid) Test
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUBackward::test_exact_vs_fast
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUBackward::test_exact_single
Uses standard library expf for numerical accuracy reference.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_forward

detaileddescription.para:
SwiGLU forward pass Test
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_forward_tokens
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_forward_single
test_mlp.py::TestMLPForward::test_swiglu_mlp
test_fused_swiglu_decode.py::TestFusedSwiGLUDecode::test_fused_swiglu_decode
test_parity.py::test_swiglu_parity
SwiGLU: y = silu(gate) * up where silu(x) = x * sigmoid(x)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *input, uint16_t *output, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_forward_bf16

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_forward_exact

detaileddescription.para:
SwiGLU forward pass (exact version using stdlib sigmoid) Test
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_exact_vs_fast
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_exact_single
Uses standard library expf for numerical accuracy reference.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *scores, int num_tokens, int n_experts, int k, int *indices, float *weights)
memberdef.name: topk_batched_f32

briefdescription.para: Batched top-K selection for multiple tokens.

parameterdescription.para: Number of experts to select per token
Output: routing weights [num_tokens, k] (can be NULL for no softmax)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *scores, int n, int k, int *indices, float *values)
memberdef.name: topk_f32

briefdescription.para: Find top-K indices and values from a score vector.

parameterdescription.para: Number of scores (e.g., number of experts)
Number of top scores to select
Output: indices of top-K scores [k], sorted descending by value
Output: top-K score values [k], sorted descending (can be NULL)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *scores, int n, int k, int *indices, float *weights)
memberdef.name: topk_softmax_f32

briefdescription.para: Find top-K indices with softmax-normalized weights.

parameterdescription.para: Number of top scores to select
Output: softmax-normalized weights for selected [k], sum to 1.0

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, const float *gamma, const float *Wq, const float *Wk, const float *Wv, float *x_norm, float *Q, float *K, float *V, int seq_len, int hidden, int q_dim, int kv_dim, float eps)

briefdescription.para: Unfused version for benchmarking comparison.

detaileddescription.para: Unfused version for benchmarking comparison.

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q6_k_q8_k

briefdescription.para: Q6_K x Q8_K dot product (single row)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const float **vectors, const float *weights, int k, int n)
memberdef.name: weighted_sum_f32

briefdescription.para: Weighted sum of k vectors: y = sum_i(weights[i] * vectors[i])

parameterdescription.para: Array of k input vector pointers, each [n]
